questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A body builder applies a force of $150 \text{ N}$ against a bullworker, compressing it by $20 \text{ cm}$. Determine the spring constant of the spring inside the bullworker.
questions.solution:
Given applied force $F = 150 \text{ N}$

Compression $x = 20 \text{ cm} = 0.2 \text{ m}$

Using Hooke's law, the magnitude of the restoring force is equal to the applied force:

$F = kx$

Substituting the given values:

$150 = k \times 0.2$

$k = \dfrac{150}{0.2} = 750 \text{ N/m}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A satellite is projected vertically upwards from an earth station. Determine the height above the earth's surface at which the force exerted on the satellite by the earth is reduced to half of its value at the earth station. (The radius of the earth is $6400 \text{ km}$.)
questions.solution:
The force exerted by the earth on the satellite at the earth's surface is given by $F = \dfrac{GMm}{R^2}$.

The force at a height $h$ above the surface is $F' = \dfrac{GMm}{(R+h)^2}$.

Given that $F' = \dfrac{F}{2}$, we have:

$\dfrac{GMm}{(R+h)^2} = \dfrac{1}{2} \dfrac{GMm}{R^2}$

$(R+h)^2 = 2R^2$

$R+h = \sqrt{2}R$

$h = (\sqrt{2} - 1)R$

Substituting $R = 6400 \text{ km}$ and $\sqrt{2} \approx 1.414$:

$h = (1.414 - 1) \times 6400$

$h = 0.414 \times 6400 = 2649.6 \text{ km}$

This is approximately $2650 \text{ km}$.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Determine the force with which you attract the earth.
questions.solution:
According to Newton's law of universal gravitation, the gravitational force between two masses $M$ and $m$ separated by a distance $r$ is given by $F = \dfrac{GMm}{r^2}$.

This force is mutual and equal in magnitude for both bodies.

The force with which the earth attracts a person is defined as the weight of the person.

By Newton's third law of motion, the person exerts an equal and opposite force on the earth.

Therefore, the force with which you attract the earth is exactly equal to your weight.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Equal charges are deposited on two spherical bodies, each having a mass of $50 \text{ kg}$ and positioned $20 \text{ cm}$ apart. If the magnitude of the Coulomb repulsion between them exactly balances their gravitational attraction, determine the magnitude of the charge on each body.
questions.solution:
The Coulomb repulsion force between the two charges is given by $F_e = \dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0} \dfrac{q^2}{r^2}$

The gravitational attraction force between the two masses is given by $F_g = G \dfrac{m^2}{r^2}$

Since the Coulomb repulsion exactly balances the gravitational attraction, we have $F_e = F_g$

$\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0} \dfrac{q^2}{r^2} = G \dfrac{m^2}{r^2}$

$q^2 = 4\pi\epsilon_0 G m^2$

$q = m \sqrt{4\pi\epsilon_0 G}$

Substituting the given values $m = 50 \text{ kg}$, $G = 6.67 \times 10^{-11} \text{ N m}^2/\text{kg}^2$, and $\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0} = 9 \times 10^9 \text{ N m}^2/\text{C}^2$:

$q = 50 \times \sqrt{\dfrac{6.67 \times 10^{-11}}{9 \times 10^9}}$

$q = 50 \times \sqrt{0.741 \times 10^{-20}}$

$q \approx 50 \times 0.86 \times 10^{-10}$

$q \approx 43 \times 10^{-10} \text{ C} = 4.3 \times 10^{-9} \text{ C}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: When placed at a separation of $20 \text{ cm}$, two charged particles exert a Coulomb force of $20 \text{ N}$ on each other. Determine the force if the separation is increased to $25 \text{ cm}$.
questions.solution:
According to Coulomb's law, the electrostatic force between two point charges is inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them, i.e., $F \propto \dfrac{1}{r^2}$.

Therefore, $\dfrac{F_2}{F_1} = \left(\dfrac{r_1}{r_2}\right)^2$

Given $F_1 = 20 \text{ N}$, $r_1 = 20 \text{ cm}$, and $r_2 = 25 \text{ cm}$.

Substituting the values:

$F_2 = 20 \times \left(\dfrac{20}{25}\right)^2$

$F_2 = 20 \times \left(\dfrac{4}{5}\right)^2$

$F_2 = 20 \times \dfrac{16}{25} = \dfrac{64}{5} = 12.8 \text{ N}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A monkey is perched on a tree limb. The limb applies a normal force of $48 \text{ N}$ and a frictional force of $20 \text{ N}$. Determine the magnitude of the total force exerted by the limb on the monkey.
questions.solution:
The total force exerted by the limb on the monkey is the resultant of the normal force and the frictional force.

Since the normal force and frictional force are perpendicular to each other, the magnitude of the total force $F$ is given by:

$F = \sqrt{N^2 + f^2}$

Substituting the given values $N = 48 \text{ N}$ and $f = 20 \text{ N}$:

$F = \sqrt{48^2 + 20^2}$

$F = \sqrt{2304 + 400}$

$F = \sqrt{2704} = 52 \text{ N}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: The gravitational force exerted on a $1 \text{ g}$ particle by a similar particle is $6.67 \times 10^{-17} \text{ N}$. Determine the separation between the particles.
questions.solution:
Given mass of each particle, $m_1 = m_2 = 1 \text{ g} = 10^{-3} \text{ kg}$

Gravitational force between the particles, $F = 6.67 \times 10^{-17} \text{ N}$

Universal gravitational constant, $G = 6.67 \times 10^{-11} \text{ N m}^2\text{kg}^{-2}$

The gravitational force is given by Newton's law of gravitation:

$F = \dfrac{G m_1 m_2}{r^2}$

Substituting the given values:

$6.67 \times 10^{-17} = \dfrac{6.67 \times 10^{-11} \times 10^{-3} \times 10^{-3}}{r^2}$

$6.67 \times 10^{-17} = \dfrac{6.67 \times 10^{-17}}{r^2}$

$r^2 = 1$

$r = 1 \text{ m}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Calculate the ratio of the magnitude of the electric force to the gravitational force exerted between two protons.
questions.solution:
The electric force between two protons separated by a distance $r$ is given by Coulomb's law:

$F_{e} = \dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_{0}} \dfrac{e^2}{r^2}$

The gravitational force between them is given by Newton's law of gravitation:

$F_{g} = G \dfrac{m_{p}^2}{r^2}$

The ratio of the electric force to the gravitational force is:

$\dfrac{F_{e}}{F_{g}} = \dfrac{e^2}{4\pi\epsilon_{0} G m_{p}^2}$

Substituting the standard values:
$e = 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \text{ C}$
$\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_{0}} = 9 \times 10^9 \text{ N m}^2\text{/C}^2$
$G = 6.67 \times 10^{-11} \text{ N m}^2\text{/kg}^2$
$m_{p} = 1.67 \times 10^{-27} \text{ kg}$

$\dfrac{F_{e}}{F_{g}} = \dfrac{9 \times 10^9 \times (1.6 \times 10^{-19})^2}{6.67 \times 10^{-11} \times (1.67 \times 10^{-27})^2}$

$\dfrac{F_{e}}{F_{g}} = \dfrac{9 \times 10^9 \times 2.56 \times 10^{-38}}{6.67 \times 10^{-11} \times 2.7889 \times 10^{-54}}$

$\dfrac{F_{e}}{F_{g}} = \dfrac{23.04 \times 10^{-29}}{18.60 \times 10^{-65}}$

$\dfrac{F_{e}}{F_{g}} \approx 1.24 \times 10^{36}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: The weight of an object is defined as the force with which the earth attracts it. Determine the weight of the moon given the following data: universal constant of gravitation $G = 6.67 \times 10^{-11} \text{ N-m}^2/\text{kg}^2$, mass of the moon $= 7.36 \times 10^{22} \text{ kg}$, mass of the earth $= 6 \times 10^{24} \text{ kg}$, and the distance between the earth and the moon $= 3.8 \times 10^{5} \text{ km}$.
questions.solution:
The weight of the moon is defined as the gravitational force exerted by the earth on the moon.

$F = \dfrac{G M_e M_m}{r^2}$

Substituting the given values:

$F = \dfrac{6.67 \times 10^{-11} \times 6 \times 10^{24} \times 7.36 \times 10^{22}}{(3.8 \times 10^8)^2}$

$F = \dfrac{294.55 \times 10^{35}}{14.44 \times 10^{16}}$

$F \approx 20.4 \times 10^{19} \text{ N} = 2.04 \times 10^{20} \text{ N}$

The closest value is $2 \times 10^{20} \text{ N}$.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: In a hydrogen atom's ground state, the average separation between the proton and the electron is $5.3 \times 10^{-11} \text{ m}$. Determine the Coulomb force between them at this separation.
questions.solution:
The Coulomb force between the proton and the electron is given by Coulomb's law:

$F = \dfrac{1}{4 \pi \epsilon_0} \dfrac{q_1 q_2}{r^2}$

Substituting the given values:

$q_1 = e = 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \text{ C}$

$q_2 = e = 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \text{ C}$

$r = 5.3 \times 10^{-11} \text{ m}$

$\dfrac{1}{4 \pi \epsilon_0} = 9 \times 10^9 \text{ N m}^2/\text{C}^2$

$F = \dfrac{9 \times 10^9 \times (1.6 \times 10^{-19})^2}{(5.3 \times 10^{-11})^2}$

$F = \dfrac{9 \times 10^9 \times 2.56 \times 10^{-38}}{28.09 \times 10^{-22}}$

$F = \dfrac{23.04 \times 10^{-29}}{28.09 \times 10^{-22}}$

$F \approx 0.82 \times 10^{-7} \text{ N}$

$F = 8.2 \times 10^{-8} \text{ N}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Determine the distance at which two charges, each equal to $1 \text{ C}$, must be placed so that the force between them is equal to your weight $W$.
questions.solution:
According to Coulomb's law, the electrostatic force between two charges $q_1$ and $q_2$ separated by a distance $r$ is given by $F = \dfrac{1}{4\pi\varepsilon_0} \dfrac{q_1 q_2}{r^2}$.

Given that $q_1 = 1 \text{ C}$, $q_2 = 1 \text{ C}$, and $F = W$.

Substituting these values into the formula, we get $W = \dfrac{1}{4\pi\varepsilon_0} \dfrac{1 \times 1}{r^2}$.

Rearranging the equation to solve for $r^2$, we obtain $r^2 = \dfrac{1}{4\pi\varepsilon_0 W}$.

Taking the square root of both sides gives $r = \sqrt{\dfrac{1}{4\pi\varepsilon_0 W}}$.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A geostationary satellite orbits at a distance of approximately $36000 \text{ km}$ from the surface of the earth. Determine the weight of a $120 \text{ kg}$ equipment placed inside this satellite. The radius of the earth is $6400 \text{ km}$.
questions.solution:
The weight of an object placed inside a satellite is its apparent weight, which is the normal reaction force exerted by the surface of the satellite on the object.

In an orbiting satellite, both the satellite and the equipment inside it are in a state of free fall towards the Earth.

The centripetal acceleration of the satellite and the equipment is provided entirely by the Earth's gravitational pull.

Let $g'$ be the acceleration due to gravity at the orbit. The equation of motion for the equipment is $mg' - N = ma_c$.

Since the satellite is in a stable orbit, $a_c = g'$.

Substituting this, we get $mg' - N = mg'$, which gives $N = 0$.

Thus, the equipment experiences weightlessness, and its weight inside the satellite is $0 \text{ N}$.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: In a hydrogen atom's ground state, the average separation between the proton and the electron is $5.3 \times 10^{-11} \text{ m}$. When the atom transitions into its first excited state, this average separation increases to four times its value in the ground state. Determine the Coulomb force in this excited state.
questions.solution:
The average separation between the proton and the electron in the first excited state is given as:

$r = 4 \times 5.3 \times 10^{-11} \text{ m} = 21.2 \times 10^{-11} \text{ m}$

The Coulomb force between the proton and the electron is given by Coulomb's law:

$F = \dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0} \dfrac{e^2}{r^2}$

Substituting the standard values $e = 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \text{ C}$ and $\dfrac{1}{4\pi\epsilon_0} = 9 \times 10^9 \text{ N m}^2/\text{C}^2$:

$F = \dfrac{9 \times 10^9 \times (1.6 \times 10^{-19})^2}{(21.2 \times 10^{-11})^2}$

$F = \dfrac{9 \times 10^9 \times 2.56 \times 10^{-38}}{449.44 \times 10^{-22}}$

$F = \dfrac{23.04 \times 10^{-29}}{449.44 \times 10^{-22}}$

$F \approx 5.1 \times 10^{-9} \text{ N}$
